markdownRemark.html:
If you work on a JavaScript application that stores data locally, think of localStorage on the web or AsyncStorage in React Native, you might have found yourself in the situation where you want to change the shape of the stored data. This on its own does not seem like an impossible task but it can get quite hairy because this might not be the first time you changed the shape of that data and not all clients out there have already been migrated to the previous version of the data. Let’s look at an example to show you what I mean.

// This is the inital shape of your data
const v0 = {
  firstName: 'Test',
  lastName: 'User'
};
